<!-- GENERATED by tradekit.policy._rules_md.write_rules_md — never hand-edit. -->
<!-- Source of truth: src/tradekit/policy/_rules.py (DESIGN §7.1). -->

# Policy rules catalog (R-001..R-016)

| ID | Applies to | WHY |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R-001 | cancel, promote, submit_order, void | Kill switch: any unresolved HaltSet denies every mutating action. Manual circuit breaker for 'something is wrong, stop everything' — the fastest gate in the catalog because it must be. Dial: none. |
| R-002 | submit_order | Promotion tier permits this account_ref + action (T0 research / T1 paper / T2 live) — an agent cannot trade live money it hasn't earned the right to touch yet (§7.3). Dial: none (tier state). |
| R-003 | submit_order | Sufficient settled balance incl. fees — an order the account cannot actually pay for is not a real trade, it's a lie the ledger would otherwise record. Dial: none (balance state). |
| R-004 | submit_order | Per-asset allowlist on live money: liquid large-caps + BTC/ETH only (SME §5) — an LLM agent does not get to discover illiquid microcaps with real dollars. Dial: allowed_assets_live. |
| R-005 | submit_order | Max position notional: live $25 flat, paper 10% of equity. Caps single-position blast radius; a small bankroll survives being wrong, not being wrong big. Dial: max_position_usd_live / max_position_pct_paper (D12). |
| R-006 | submit_order | Max total live exposure across all open live positions — a portfolio-level cap so no combination of individually-legal orders can compound into a bankroll-ending exposure. Dial: max_total_live_exposure_usd (D12). |
| R-007 | submit_order | Daily trade count cap — deliberately tighter than 'unlimited' for anti-gaming reasons, cheap to dial up later. Dial: max_daily_trades_live / max_daily_trades_paper. |
| R-008 | submit_order | Min notional $10 — blocks micro-trade series gaming and fee-noise grading (SME §5): ten $2 trades cannot manufacture a clean series. Dial: min_notional_usd. |
| R-009 | submit_order | Drawdown circuit breaker: account drawdown >= 10% (30d peak) blocks new positions until a graded review — applies to advisory too (F7), because a losing streak is exactly when an agent should stop compounding it. Dial: drawdown_breaker_pct. |
| R-010 | submit_order | Thesis prerequisites: approved review artifact + numeric EV block + market snapshot must all be present — a submission with no falsifiable, reviewed thesis behind it is not a trade the system can grade honestly. Dial: none. |
| R-011 | submit_order | Live sequence budget: a T2 promotion grants exactly 3 live trades, then auto-reverts to review — live money is a probation, not a permanent unlock. Dial: live_sequence_budget. |
| R-012 | submit_order | Sizing purity (F6): the submitted order size must match the recorded mae.size_position output for this thesis within tolerance — an agent cannot revenge-size past what its own sizing model computed. Dial: sizing_tolerance_pct. |
| R-013 | submit_order | Correlation cap: a new position with \|r\| > 0.75 to an open position is not real diversification, it's the same bet twice — requires an explicit review flag before it can proceed. Dial: correlation_cap. |
| R-014 | submit_order | Advisory cooling-off: manual trades over $200 need the backing thesis to be at least 24h old — a guardrail against same-day impulse trades dressed up as theses (F-guardrail 5). Dial: cooling_off_notional_usd / cooling_off_hours. |
| R-015 | submit_order | VOID-rate audit: more than 20% voids in the trailing 20 graded theses blocks new submissions until a process review — voiding is for genuine structural invalidation, not a way to erase losers before they hurt a series. Dial: void_rate_window / void_rate_cap_pct (§10.4). |
| R-016 | promote | Promotion metric gates: the strategy acceptance table (§9.4) must pass at promotion evaluation — a good win rate on a coin flip is not evidence of edge. Dial: none (per-metric thresholds live in mae.compute_strategy_metrics; P2 reads a stubbed summary — batch D wires the real computation, FLAGGED SEAM). |
